title: Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani

seo.title: Classic Butter Chicken Recipe (Murgh Makhani)
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of classic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) with our authentic Indian recipe. Creamy, rich, and flavorful, it's easier to make at home than you think! Get the full recipe.
h2Headings: What is Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani?
Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: A vibrant bowl of classic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) with creamy sauce, garnished with cilantro.
seo.imageCaption: Savor the authentic taste of India with our Classic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) recipe.

description: Butter Chicken, or Murgh Makhani, is a beloved Indian curry known for its rich, creamy tomato-based sauce and tender pieces of marinated chicken. This classic dish offers a harmonious blend of sweet, tangy, and subtly spiced flavors, making it a globally popular comfort food. Our authentic recipe guides you to create this restaurant-quality masterpiece right in your own kitchen.

ingredients.item: Boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breast

ingredients.notes: soaked in warm water for 15 minutes

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine chicken pieces with yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, red chili powder, turmeric powder, garam masala, lemon juice, and salt. Mix well to coat all chicken pieces. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or preferably 2-4 hours for best flavor.

instructions.title: Cook the Chicken
instructions.text: Heat 1 tablespoon of oil in a large pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces in a single layer, ensuring not to overcrowd the pan. Cook for 3-4 minutes per side until lightly browned and cooked through. Remove the chicken and set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Sauce Base
instructions.text: In the same pan, melt 2 tablespoons of butter over medium heat. Add the whole spices (cardamom, cinnamon, cloves) and let them sizzle for 30 seconds until fragrant. Add the chopped onion and cook until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes.

instructions.title: Build the Flavor
instructions.text: Add ginger-garlic paste and green chilies (if using) and cook for 1 minute until aromatic. Stir in the ground spices: coriander powder, cumin powder, red chili powder, turmeric powder, and 1/4 teaspoon garam masala. Cook for 30 seconds, stirring constantly, to toast the spices.

instructions.title: Simmer and Blend
instructions.text: Pour in the crushed tomatoes, soaked cashews, and 1/2 cup water. Bring to a simmer, then cover and cook for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the tomatoes have softened and the oil begins to separate. Remove from heat and let it cool slightly. Carefully transfer the mixture to a blender and blend until completely smooth. You may need to do this in batches.

instructions.title: Finish the Makhani Sauce
instructions.text: Strain the blended sauce through a fine-mesh sieve back into the pan to ensure a silky-smooth texture. Discard any solids. Bring the strained sauce to a gentle simmer over medium-low heat. Add sugar and salt to taste. Stir in the crushed kasuri methi.

instructions.title: Combine and Serve
instructions.text: Add the cooked chicken pieces to the simmering sauce. Stir well to coat the chicken. Cook for another 5-7 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the chicken to heat through. Finally, stir in the heavy cream. Do not boil after adding cream. Garnish with fresh chopped cilantro and a swirl of extra cream. Serve hot with naan, roti, or basmati rice.

tips: For the smoothest sauce, always blend the tomato-cashew mixture thoroughly and then pass it through a fine-mesh sieve. This removes any fibrous bits and ensures a restaurant-quality texture.
Kashmiri red chili powder provides a beautiful deep red color without excessive heat. If unavailable, use a mix of regular red chili powder and a pinch of paprika for color.
Avoid boiling the curry vigorously after adding the heavy cream, as it can curdle. Keep it on a gentle simmer until just heated through for a perfectly creamy finish.
article:
**Savor the Silky Smoothness: Your Guide to Classic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani)**

Few dishes command as much global adoration and culinary curiosity as Butter Chicken, known in its native India as Murgh Makhani. This iconic Indian chicken recipe, celebrated for its rich, creamy texture and complex yet comforting flavors, has transcended borders to become a staple on menus worldwide. Whether you’re a seasoned chef or a home cook eager to explore the depths of Indian cuisine, mastering this classic dish is a rewarding endeavor. Prepare to embark on a flavorful journey as we unveil the secrets to creating the perfect Butter Chicken, a dish that truly embodies the warmth and spice of India.

## What is Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani?

At its heart, classic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) is a testament to the art of balancing flavors. Originating in Delhi in the 1950s, this dish was reputedly created by Kundan Lal Jaggi and Kundan Lal Gujral at Moti Mahal restaurant. It began as a clever way to use leftover tandoori chicken, simmering it in a rich tomato-based gravy. The name "Murgh Makhani" literally translates to "chicken with butter," an apt description for a dish that generously incorporates butter and cream, lending it an unparalleled silky texture and decadent mouthfeel.

The defining characteristics of this beloved curry are its vibrant orange-red hue, its luscious, velvety gravy, and the tender, often smoky, pieces of chicken. The flavor profile is a harmonious blend of tangy tomatoes, sweet cream, aromatic spices like garam masala and fenugreek (kasoori methi), and a subtle underlying smokiness. It’s a dish that is deeply satisfying without being overly spicy, making it a favorite for all palates.

## Ingredients You Need

Crafting an authentic Butter Chicken requires a careful selection of fresh ingredients. Here’s a comprehensive list to get you started:

**For the Chicken Marinade:**
*   500g boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breast, cut into 1-inch pieces
*   1/2 cup plain yogurt
*   1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste
*   1 teaspoon red chili powder (Kashmiri chili powder for color)
*   1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder
*   1 teaspoon garam masala
*   1/2 teaspoon cumin powder
*   Salt to taste

**For the Makhani Gravy:**
*   2 tablespoons unsalted butter
*   1 tablespoon oil (vegetable or canola)
*   1 large onion, finely chopped
*   1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste
*   1 can (400g) crushed tomatoes or 3-4 ripe fresh tomatoes, pureed
*   1/4 cup raw cashews (soaked in hot water for 15 minutes, then drained)
*   1 teaspoon red chili powder (adjust to your spice preference)
*   1 teaspoon coriander powder
*   1/2 teaspoon cumin powder
*   1/2 teaspoon garam masala
*   1 teaspoon kasoori methi (dried fenugreek leaves), crushed
*   1/2 cup heavy cream (plus extra for garnish)
*   1-2 tablespoons sugar or honey (to balance acidity)
*   Salt to taste
*   1/2 cup water or chicken broth (as needed for gravy consistency)

**For Garnish:**
*   Fresh cilantro, chopped
*   Extra swirl of heavy cream

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Follow these steps to create a truly unforgettable Butter Chicken:

1.  **Marinate the Chicken:** In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with all marinade ingredients. Mix well, ensuring the chicken is thoroughly coated. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or ideally 2-4 hours, or even overnight for best flavor.

2.  **Cook the Chicken:** Heat a tablespoon of oil in a large pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken in a single layer, working in batches if necessary to avoid overcrowding. Cook until nicely browned and almost cooked through, about 5-7 minutes per side. Set aside. (For an authentic smoky flavor, you can char the chicken slightly under a broiler or on a grill.)

3.  **Prepare the Gravy Base:** In the same pan, melt the butter over medium heat. Add the chopped onions and sauté until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the ginger-garlic paste and cook for another minute until fragrant.

4.  **Build the Gravy:** Stir in the crushed tomatoes, soaked cashews, red chili powder, coriander powder, and cumin powder. Cook for 8-10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the tomatoes have softened and the mixture thickens slightly.

5.  **Blend the Gravy:** Carefully transfer the tomato-cashew mixture to a blender. Add about 1/4 cup of water or broth. Blend until completely smooth and creamy. For an extra silky texture, you can pass the blended gravy through a fine-mesh sieve, pressing down to extract all the liquid.

6.  **Finish the Gravy:** Return the smooth gravy to the pan. Add the garam masala, crushed kasoori methi, sugar (or honey), and salt. Stir well and bring to a gentle simmer.

7.  **Combine and Simmer:** Gently fold in the cooked chicken pieces into the gravy. Add the heavy cream and stir until combined. If the gravy is too thick, add a little more water or broth to reach your desired consistency. Simmer for another 5-10 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the chicken to finish cooking through.

8.  **Garnish and Serve:** Taste and adjust seasonings if necessary. Garnish with fresh chopped cilantro and a swirl of extra heavy cream.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

Achieving that signature Butter Chicken flavor and texture requires attention to a few key details:

*   **Marination is Key:** Don't skimp on marination time. The longer the chicken marinates, the more tender and flavorful it will be.
*   **Char the Chicken:** While not strictly necessary, lightly charring the chicken (either by pan-frying until well-browned, grilling, or even using a small piece of hot charcoal in the gravy for a smoky finish) adds an authentic depth of flavor that elevates the dish.
*   **Smooth Gravy:** Blending the gravy until absolutely smooth is crucial for the classic silky texture. Don't rush this step. Passing it through a sieve can make a noticeable difference.
*   **Balance Sweet and Tangy:** Tomatoes can vary in acidity. Use sugar or honey to balance the tanginess of the tomatoes, creating a harmonious sweet and savory profile. Taste and adjust.
*   **Kasoori Methi:** Don't skip the kasoori methi! Toasting it lightly in a dry pan for a few seconds before crushing and adding it to the gravy enhances its unique, slightly bitter, aromatic flavor.
*   **Quality Ingredients:** High-quality butter, fresh cream, and good-quality spices make a significant difference in the final taste of your Butter Chicken.

## Serving Suggestions

Butter Chicken is a versatile dish that pairs wonderfully with a variety of accompaniments. Here are some classic serving suggestions:

*   **Naan Bread:** Soft, chewy naan (plain, garlic, or butter naan) is perfect for scooping up every last drop of the rich gravy.
*   **Basmati Rice:** Steamed basmati rice or fragrant jeera rice (cumin rice) provides a light contrast to the richness of the curry.
*   **Roti or Paratha:** Other Indian breads like tandoori roti or flaky paratha also make excellent choices.
*   **Side Salad:** A simple kachumber salad (cucumber, onion, tomato with a lemon dressing) offers a refreshing crunch to cut through the richness.
*   **Raita:** A cooling yogurt-based raita (cucumber or mint) can provide a soothing counterpoint to the spice.

## Variations and Substitutions

While this recipe focuses on classic Butter Chicken, there are many ways to adapt it to dietary needs or personal preferences:

*   **Vegetarian Butter Paneer/Tofu:** Substitute the chicken with cubes of paneer (Indian cheese) or firm tofu. Marinate and pan-fry them similarly, then add to the gravy.
*   **Dairy-Free:** For a dairy-free version, use coconut oil instead of butter, and substitute heavy cream with full-fat coconut milk or a cashew cream blended from soaked cashews and water.
*   **Spice Level:** Adjust the amount of red chili powder to suit your preferred spice level. For more heat, add a pinch of cayenne pepper or a chopped green chili.
*   **Smoky Flavor:** To achieve a deeper smoky flavor without a tandoor, after adding the chicken to the gravy, create a small well. Place a small piece of burning charcoal in a small metal bowl, drizzle a teaspoon of ghee over it, and quickly cover the pan with a lid for 5-10 minutes.

**Embrace the Journey to Culinary Delight**

Butter Chicken is more than just a dish; it's a culinary experience that brings joy and comfort with every bite. By following this detailed guide, you're not just cooking; you're recreating a piece of Indian culinary heritage in your own kitchen. The effort is truly worth it for the incredible aroma and the exquisite taste of homemade classic Butter Chicken. So gather your ingredients, follow these steps, and prepare to delight your senses with this universally cherished Indian masterpiece.
